perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE VII FEES Section 7.1 Access Fee (a) Digital Screen Usage Fee. On or before sixty (60) days after the end of each month during the Term, NCM shall pay to Network Affiliate $30.00 per digitized screen (i.e., a screen connected to the Digital Content Network), provided Network Affiliate provides the required attendance and screen count information as such information time frames are established by NCM on a monthly basis.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE VII FEES Section 7.1 Access Fee (a) Digital Screen Usage Fee. NCM may pay to Network Affiliate a discretionary amount per digitized screen (i.e., a screen connected to the Digital Content Network), provided Network Affiliate provides the required attendance and screen count information as such information time frames are established by NCM on a monthly basis.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'on or before sixty days' payment term creates an omission that contradicts prompt payment laws. Payment terms are now at NCM's discretion, thus weakening the Affiliate's rights and introducing legal risks.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Prompt Payment laws exist at the state level.
perturbation.law_explanation: New Jersey's Prompt Payment Act mandates specific payment timelines for construction projects, and this omission could be interpreted as a circumvention of those guidelines if the agreement is related to construction or improvements of the theatre. Making the payment terms 'discretionary' directly conflicts with the requirement of timely payment.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: New Jersey Revised Statutes Section 2A:30A-2 (2023) - Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. :: 2023 New Jersey Revised Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 N.J. Rev. Stat. 2023 N.J. Rev. Stat. (here) 2022 N.J. Rev. Stat. View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2023 New Jersey Revised Statutes Title 2A - Administration of Civil and Criminal Justice Section 2A:30A-2 - Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. Universal Citation: NJ Rev Stat § 2A:30A-2 (2023) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next 2A:30A-2 Payment to prime contractor, subcontractor, subsubcontractor, timely payment; exceptions; disputes; resolution. 2. a. If a prime contractor has performed in accordance with the provisions of a contract with the owner and the billing for the work has been approved and certified by the owner or the owner's authorized approving agent, the owner shall pay the amount due to the prime contractor for each periodic payment, final payment or retainage monies not more than 30 calendar days after the billing date, which for a periodic billing, shall be the periodic billing date specified in the contract. The billing shall be deemed approved and certified 20 days after the owner receives it unless the owner provides, before the end of the 20-day period, a written statement of the amount withheld and the reason for withholding payment, except that in the case of a public or governmental entity that requires the entity's governing body to vote on authorizations for each periodic payment, final payment or retainage monies, the amount due may be approved and certified at the next scheduled public meeting of the entity's governing body, and paid during the entity's subsequent paymen
perturbation.justification: The modification changes a mandatory payment term ('shall pay') to a discretionary one ('may pay'), removing a legally required obligation. The 'Perturbation Explanation' accurately reflects the change as weakening the Affiliate's rights. The New Jersey Revised Statutes Section 2A:30A-2, as shown in 'Scraped Law Snippet 1', mandates timely payment to contractors after work is approved and billed. While the snippet focuses on construction, the principle of 'timely payment' is legally significant and broadly applicable. By making payment discretionary, the changed text contradicts the spirit and potential application of prompt payment laws, even if the specific context isn't explicitly construction-related. The omission of a defined payment timeline introduces legal risk.

perturbation.original_text: Network Affiliate shall maintain with financially sound and reputable insurance companies insurance on the Theatres and the Equipment in such amounts and against such perils as Network Affiliate deems adequate for its business. NCM shall maintain with financially sound and reputable insurance companies insurance for its business and Equipment in such amounts and against such perils as NCM deems adequate for its business, including the installation services set forth in Section 2.2 herein. Each Party will name the other Party (including its agents, officers, directors, employees and affiliates) as an additional insured on such policies of insurance.
perturbation.changed_text: Network Affiliate shall maintain with financially sound and reputable insurance companies insurance on the Theatres and the Equipment. NCM shall maintain with financially sound and reputable insurance companies insurance for its business and Equipment. Each Party may name the other Party as an additional insured on such policies of insurance.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'in such amounts and against such perils as Network Affiliate deems adequate for its business' weakens the requirement that the policy must be adequate to ensure the equipment is secure. Removing the requirement that each party 'will' name the other as additionally insured removes an important requirement for compliance. There are also state insurance regulations in New Jersey, where Digital Cinema Destinations Corp. is located (see Section 14.1) that this change may conflict with.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Insurance regulations often stipulate minimum coverage requirements to protect third parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts insurance laws because it eliminates the guarantee that the insurance amounts will be adequate, and omits the requirement for each party to be named as an additional insurer, potentially not covering liability. The section NJ Admin Code 11:2-17.7 describes unfair practices relating to insurance, which is a risk this modification would introduce.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that each party 'will name' the other as an additional insured, making it a mandatory requirement. The changed text replaces 'will name' with 'may name', making it optional. Additionally, the original text specifies 'in such amounts and against such perils as Network Affiliate deems adequate for its business'. Removing this clause weakens the requirement that the policy must be adequate to ensure the equipment is secure. The Perturbation Explanation indicates that these changes may conflict with state insurance regulations. The Law Explanation supports this by suggesting that these changes potentially fail to cover liability. Though Scraped Law Snippets are absent, the substantial weakening of the insurance requirements and the removal of the mandatory naming of additional insured parties create a significant contradiction, because legally mandated consumer protections are now omitted, fitting the Omission Legal Contradiction definition.
